contract_scope: Optional graph-aware metadata layer on top of the S-batch semantic discovery core. Closes readiness tracker bullets W.8 (graph-aware metadata + relationship traversal) and W.9 (public contract boundary between core semantic discovery and optional graph integrations).

core_surface.authoritative_ranking: cosine similarity over service_embeddings / tool_embeddings plus optional BM25 fusion with alpha
core_surface.guarantee: Any /api/discover call WITHOUT the optional graph_expand field produces identical output (bytes and ranking) to pre-G-batch behaviour. The core result set is never re-ordered by the graph layer.

shape.edge_types: optional array<string> (0..16 items, each 1..64 chars, regex [a-z][a-z0-9_.-]*); null/omitted = any edge type
shape.max_hops: optional int in [1, 2]; default 1; hard-capped at 2 for demo scale

optional_extension.effect: After the core scorer returns its ranked results, the server walks outgoing service_edges from each top-N service_id, collects unique neighbor service_ids (seeds excluded), and appends them to the response under `expanded` with source="graph_expand" and semantic_score=null. Neighbors never mutate or re-rank the authoritative `results` array.

honesty_rules: The graph layer stores directional edges only. Undirected relationships must be modeled as two edges (A→B, B→A) with matching edge_type if the caller wants symmetric traversal.
Edge weight / scoring is NOT implemented. Traversal is a plain BFS bounded by max_hops and a fixed visit budget (512) to prevent runaway expansion.

The demo does not ship a seeded graph — callers register their own edges via a future admin endpoint or directly against the service_edges table. Edge registration via HTTP is a future hardening leaf; out of scope for #G-batch.
This extension does NOT close MoE / expert-routing fences (tracker V.5). Expert routing is a semantically different problem (token-level routing inside the model) and stays fenced.
